title: Understanding Bootstrapping
text: Increasing the number of bootstrap samples makes the confidence interval endpoints more stable which increases precision. For example, using $$500$$ resamples might give intervals like $$[2.06$$, $$3.45]$$ or $$[2.13$$, $$3.38]$$, while using 100,000 resamples might repeatedly give intervals close to $$[2.10$$, $$3.40]$$ However, this does not make the interval much narrower. To reduce confidence interval width, we need a greater original sample size, not more bootstrap resamples.
